title: Damascus
column8: northernmost city of the Decapolis and a major commercial hub.

title: Gerasa (Jerash)
description: One of the best preserved Roman provincial towns in the world

title: Scythopolis
description: The capital of the Decapolis league and only city located west of the Jordan River.

title: Gadara (Umm Qais)
description: Famous for its views over the Sea of Galilee and home to several philosophers.

title: Hippos (Sussita)
description: Located on a diamond-shaped plateau above the Sea of Galilee with massive fortifications.

title: Pella
description: A vital archaeological site showing continuous habitation from the Neolithic to the Islamic era.

title: Philadelphia (Amman)
column8: named after Ptolemy II Philadelphus of Egypt.

title: Capitolias (Beit Ras)
column8: it contains a well-preserved Roman theater.

title: Canatha (Qanawat)
description: A mountain city in modern Syria known for its "Helios" temple and massive cisterns.

title: Abila
column8: famous for its elaborate tombs and water tunnels.

title: Dion (Dium)
description: A city whose location was long debated
column8: likely Tell al-Ash'ari in modern Syria.
